content: I'm feeling really overwhelmed trying to balance work and taking care of my parents in Raleigh. I barely have time for my own hobbies anymore.
That sounds incredibly challenging. How old are your parents and what kind of care do they need?
My dad is 72 and has early-stage Parkinson's, and my mom is 68 with some mobility issues after her hip surgery last year. I try to visit them every evening after work.
That's a lot to manage. When was the last time you did something just for yourself?
Honestly, it's been months. I used to go to my archery club twice a week, but I haven't picked up my bow since January. I miss it so much.
It's important to maintain your own interests. Have you considered asking other family members for help so you can have some time for yourself?
I have a sister in Charlotte, but she works weekends at the hospital. We're planning a small family gathering next month though - my first real break in ages.
That's great that you'll have some family time! Where are you planning to have this gathering?
At my parents' house. I'm hoping to get everyone together for a Sunday afternoon - maybe do a potluck since I'll be too exhausted to cook for everyone.
A potluck is a smart idea. Will you be inviting anyone from work or your other social circles?
I was thinking of inviting my friend Marcus from the stand-up comedy group. He's been trying to get me to come to open mic nights, but I haven't had the energy.
Marcus sounds like a good friend. Does he know about your situation with your parents?
Yeah, he's been really understanding. Actually, he suggested I start a travel blog about my trips before all this started - I used to travel so much more.
A travel blog could be a great creative outlet! Do you still play piano? That used to be one of your passions, right?
I do, but my keyboard has been gathering dust. I barely have time to practice anymore, which is sad because I was getting pretty good at Chopin's nocturnes.
It's tough when life gets so busy. Have you thought about setting aside even just 15 minutes a day for piano or archery?
I know I should. Maybe I can start with just weekends. The family gathering might be a good time to dust off the keyboard and play something for everyone.
